Ab itna time admit hone ke bad koi banda ghar jaane ke liye Kitna paranoid rahata hai usko agar aap दो-तीन din bhi jyada rokto 2 saal Jaise mahsus hote hain to bhi yeh  kaise delay karva rahe the ki

​ @GyanTherapy If this happens it's hospital's negligence. Jab aap hospital jaate ho to start me wo ek claim intimation de dete hai insurance company ko jo end amount se bohot kam hota hai but ek baar uska approval mil jaye to more aur less end amount bhi approve ho hi jayega.

Isme administration aur biomedical wastage (gloves, syringe etc.) ke charges nahi pay hote hai.

Rakesh bhai ne jo tie-up ki baat ki agar wo hai to kabhi kabhi hospital insurance company ko discount de deta hai aur agar wo insurance company achhi ho to wo tumhe pass kr deti hai jisse aap jo end amount pay krte ho wo aur kam ho skta hai.

Please share this idea of investment that I am doing it right now after getting parashan of these health insurances companies.. if salaries employees then they will have cover by company, then let them invest small portion like 1000-2000 in sip till their retirement that fund will be more to cover there hospital bill if started from age 30. Yes, My Bade Papa had a small laser operation of kidney stone which was done on cashless scheme. When everything was done and we opted for discharge,they refuted to discharge by saying that the insurance company hadn't initiated the claimed amount!

comments.text: @debiprasadpradhan5118  This is what happens in a low trust society where everyone is trying to cheat each other. Normal people are trying to cheat insurance companies by creating fake bills. Hospitals are trying to cheat insurance companies by inflating bill with excessive tests and overcharging normal treatments. In return, insurance companies are trying to cheat customers by rejecting genuine claims. Honest people get stuck in the crossfire and suffer endlessly. There is no hope for this country.
comments.author: @anupdev5845

comments.text:
Not just the discharge of the patient. With cashless, the hospital gets a free hand to choose treatment procedure / material / equipment as they please. Where a needle of 20 rupees can do the work, they will use a needle of 200 rupees. They do unwanted tests, unwanted procedures which is very bad for patients. I know IAF retired official, who went for his wife's blood test (simple blood profiling), which was to be done at a hospital covered under CGHS I believe (I may be wrong but he was covered under armed forces medical treatment scheme). She was admitted there for 2 days just for the blood test!!! In the bill copy they charged for doctor visit, bed charge, food, nurse charge etc. 

In my native city, those hospitals are under CGHS scheme, where central govt employees will get cashless treatment. But I know those hospitals well. Cashless or no cashless, I would not step my foot in those hospitals... Hospital - insurance - government is the biggest nexus to loot people.
comments.author: @saketsuman13

comments.text: My experience with Health Insurance is this industry is an organised Scam that is cheating the masses. They sell insurance with false promises of cashless reimbursements and other false promises - In reality their main goal is to find ways and means to deny paying the customer their legitimate claims .  There also seems to be a nexus between the hospitals and these Insurance companies in most cases.  How the Indian Govt and their regulatory bodies are allowing this to go on unchecked is very surprising .

BEWARE of NIVA BUPA.....They are the most FRAUDESTER company in Medicla insurance. They will never pay you if your claim amount is high. Recently my mother (68 years) got heart attack and treatment was taken in Fortis Hospital but they rejected my claim asking that once she had Arthritis 22 years ago and they need that Prescription of Doctor as hard copy. I explained that at time she had taken medication only for a month, she got relief and never has taken medication since then, also how can any one keep a Doctors prescription for 22 years, but they were adament on asking the 22 years ago prescription and they rejected my claim of 4.5 lacs on that ground. Infact with each day and my follow up they began to raise new request asking for Diabetes First prescription given by Dr. in 2015 (however diabetes was declared in policy), asking for Dengue prescription once she had in 2009.
I visited their office and found people that they settle big claims 1/100 otherwise they raise query in 99% cases of big amount. If you claim amount is equal or twice the premium amount you are paying then it is okay for them to settle the claim, but if your claim amount is higher or equal to policy limit then even GOD will not save you from NIVA BUPA.
They know that my mother is in elderly age and the claim of 4,50 lacs will be paid in 8 premium amount and that too if every thing goes okay with my mother in future, as such playing on a safer side NIVA BUPA choose the way to escape by raising unrealistic query to my claim.
After a long harrassment by them I asked them to cancel my policy and give my premium balance left in a year ( I paid the premium thrice before for same policy of RS. 65000 every year), but they denied this refund also to me. If any one had any doubt I can present the proof to them. 
So, I decided to expose them on every social platform so that no other person shall get befooled by them.
I will recommend people that if your claim is twice the limit of your premium then it is okay for the company to pay you but if you think that your policy is of 15 lacs and you go to the Hospital thinking you have nothing to pay as your Insurance company will take care of you, then you are COMPLETELY WRONG. You have to pay from your pocket and you will be Debt. So instead of paying these company I wil recommend to save the premium amount to your bankers and get them Fixed deposit and use it when required. In either case you have to suffer then why to suffer because of some third person as you will feel more robbed as you have paid twice once you had paid to insurance company and secondly you have paid to Hospital.
Please BEWARE of NIVA BUPA

comments.text:
Please Help !!!

Mera bhi Care Health Insurance ne claim reject kar diya.
Mere papa cancer patient hain, lekin insurance company ne galat tareeke se hamara claim reject kar diya, yeh kehkar ki patient tobacco khate hain.

Humne unhe doctor ka certificate diya, jisme clearly likha tha ki papa ne kabhi bhi tobacco ya pan masala ka istemal nahi kiya hai. Phir bhi company ne 3 din tak wait karwaaya, baar-baar documents maange, aur aakhir mein bina kisi valid reason ke claim reject kar diya.

Ab tak ka kharcha 3.5 lakh ka kharcha ho gaya hai jo maine bhara hai ,  aur aage bhi chemotherapy ke liye treatment cost bahut zyada hai. Humne kai baar unse baat karne ki koshish ki, unhe sab documents dikhaye, lekin unhone ek baar bhi meri baat ko seriously nahi liya. Sirf wait karne ko kehte rahe, aur baad mein wahi jhooti wajah deke claim reject kar diya.

Yeh sirf galat service nahi hai, yeh fraud, patient ke sath mental harassment aur trust todne wala kaam hai. Itne bade health crisis mein bhi agar insurance company support na kare, toh phir insurance ka matlab kya reh jaata hai?

Main aapse vinamrta se nivedan karta hoon ki meri madad karein. Company ko sahi direction dijiye taaki hamara claim approve ho, aur aage ka treatment smoothly ho sake. Aise galat practices ke khilaf action lena zaroori hai, warna aur bhi families ko yeh jhelna padega.

comments.text:
हेल्थ इन्सयोरेन्स का वीभत्स चेहरा और उसका काला संसार
असली गलती हॉस्पिटल्स की ही है बाद में इंसयोरेन्स कंपनी की । इनकी मनमानी वसूली और अंधाधुंध कमाई रुक नही रही है । अस्पताल लूट खसोट का अड्डा बन गए हैं । कैशलेस वालो का  गैर जरूरी इलाज किया जारहा है ,गैर जरूरी जांचे की जारही है । अनाप शनाप बिलिंग की जा रही है । इंसयोरेन्स कंपनी और अस्पताल दोनों के लिए एक व्यक्ति मरीज नही मुर्गा है जिसे बस हलाल करना है । अस्पतालों और डॉक्टरों के अंदर जबरदस्त लालच और लोभ घर कर के बैठा है । कोई भले ही सड़क पर आ जाये पर इनकी इमारतें खड़ी होती रहनी चाहिए । वास्तव में इलाज इतना महंगा नही है बस लालच बड़ा है ।  इंसयोरेन्स कंपनी पैसे लेते वक्त तो आपकी दुनिया की सबसे बड़ी हितैषी बन जाती है पर देते समय इतना परेशान कर देती है कि मरीज के साथ साथ घर के दूसरे लोग भी बीमार हो जाते है । ये दोनों सेवा प्रदाता बनने के बजाय लूट खसोट का धंधा चलाने वाले रैकेट की तरह काम कर रहे है । स्वास्थ्य, सुरक्षा शिक्षा और पोषण ये चार चीज़े हर व्यक्ति की बुनियादी जरूरत है और इन्हें सरकार को पूरी तरह से अपने नियंत्रण में रखना चाहिए । पर सरकार में बैठे लोग अपने निजी स्वार्थ के कारण मंदिर और धर्म स्थानों का नियंत्रण तो अपने हाथों में रखते है पर वास्तव में जहां सरकारी नियंत्रण और शक्तिशाली हस्तक्षेप की आवश्यकता है वहा भेड़ियों , माफिया और राक्षसों को नंगा नाच करने के लिए पूरी तरह से खुला छोड़ दिया है । यदि अस्पतालों में इतना लूट खसोट न होता तो हेल्थ इंसयोरेन्स वाले इतने सारे कुकुरमुत्ते न उगते । सरकार तो कोरोना काल मे ही इन प्राइवेट अस्पतालों,डॉक्टरो और इंसयोरेन्स कंपनियों की सच्चाई जान चुकी थी पर अभी तक कोई ठोस कदम नही उठाया ,आश्चर्य है । इससे सरकार की मंशा पर संदेह उत्पन्न होता है ।

comments.text:
Bhai m ek popular health insurance company me job krta hu, company 1st or 4th quarter me jayda claim pass krti h,2nd or 3rd quarter me bhut kam claim pass hote h,or agar company jb claim pass krti h tb consumables k naam se bhut jayda deductions krti h, customer se 3 year ka premium lene k liye 10% discount ka lalach diya jata h or usi me customer fas jata h kyuki vo dusri company me port bhi nhi kr skta,or claim settlement alag alag zone dekh kr pass krti h,jis zone me company profit me hogi vha ki claim settlement jayda hoti h or jis zone me company loss me hoti h vha se claim rejection bhut jayda hota h.
Kuch points hmesha yaad rakhne chiye claim lene se pehle 
1 Insurance agent se he le, online na le
2 company ka background dekh kr le
3 panel hospital ki list check kre
4 treatment hmesha cashless hospital me kraye 
5 policy hmesa emi pr he leni chiye,agar aapka claim reject hota h to aap dusri company me port krva skte h

comments.text: Hospital aur Health insurance  dono mil kar public ko pelte hain. Koi bhi insurance company ho ya koi bhi hospital ho.......ek bar inke hath lag gaye beta fir dekho kese ragadatey hain. Care health ka policy 3 saal pehle ye keh kar diya gaya tha ki pan india aap ko kahi bhi medical facility di jayegi aur apke city me to lagbhag har hospital hamaray panel pe hain to aap jaha sahi lagay waha ilaaj karwayein... premium ka payment ho gaya policy mil gaya..1 maheene baaad email ana shuru ho gaya ki pan india lagbhag 250-300 hospital  panel se hata diye gaye hain .. fir 2 maheeney bad ek aur email ata hai fir se 100-200 hospital panel se gayab kar diye gaye aur aaj tak sirf yahi email ata hai ki bhai ab tumhare liye 2 -4 hospital hi bache hain jaha ka claim hum deingay (agar hamari marji hui to.) Mai heran hun ki sarkar bhi mon hai is dhandliyo pe. Koi naya kanoon koi policy nahi banai jati. Medical ka kharcha har saal 20 se 30% badh jata hai jo dawa 2 sal pehle 30 rupye me milti thi aaj wahi dawa 120 - 130 rupye me milti hai. Jis doctor ki fees 400 thi aaj wo 1000 lete hai. Akhir kaha tak jayega ye loot maar ka dhanda. Gareeb insan ke liye dawa daru to sab band. Marte ho to mar jao saley...jee kar bhi kya karogay tum.....tumko jeenay ka koi adhikar nahi. aur jo bechare kisi tarah mota mota premium bhar bhi dete hain unkko koi gaurantee nahi ki gaand fatne pe koi patti dettol bhi lagaega ya nahi...Haan CARE insurance ka  Policy Renewal ka mail kabhi miss nahi hota...Email whatsapp phone call sms har trah se aap ko yaad karate hain ki tel le ke tyar ho jao tumhari gaand marene ka time aa gya aa rahe hain. SORRY FOR SOME OF MY dirty derogatory words lekin kya karu..........kaleja jalta hai jab har saal premium bhi do fir claim lene ke time mein inko hath per jodo.

comments.text:
Just follow these-
1.Buy from any PSU healthcare insurance company which is nearest to your home.

2.Buy though agent only.2-3% extra charge may be added but it matters during emergency.I repeat only offline through office.

3.Fill proposal from yourself only.Disclose all truth regarding health condition

4.Avail TPA for cashless claim.

5.Save Agent's mobile no and Branch's no in your mobile and inform to your family members.

6.Try to buy sum insured 5 lakh or above.atleast 3 lakh + with sum restoration add on.

Rest will be taken care by the Govt.Have a happy life.

N.B-I have health insurance from UIIC.Wonderful experience from them for 3 claims already settled with 80-85% cost return with cashless facility.

comments.text:
Hello, I have a question and need your suggestion. This is directed towards anyone who can answer it correctly. 

My father is 64. 
He was diagnosed with High Blood Pressure in 2021 January. 
In September 2021 he had a mild Cerebral Stroke, was hospitalized for 8 days and didn't have any long term effect. 

Right now he takes BP medicine that keeps his blood pressure in check and doesn't have any other issue. 

Previously his company used to take care of any health related issues, when we used to live in Kolkata. But we have recently moved to Hyderabad because of my job and that is no longer an option. 
 
I have applied for a HDFC ERGO Optima Secure plan a few weeks earlier, but HDFC refused to issue the policy stating that if a senior citizen had a stroke as per the IRDAI guidelines he/she can not be insured by any insurer. 

I need to understand, how true is that. And if it is at all possible to get a health insurance for my father. 

My father has done everything humanly possible to make sure that I have a better life than he ever had and now I want to make his life better in whichever way possible.   

Any help is appreciated.
comments.author: @johnny19100

comments.text:
Hi Rakesh Bhai,

I want to highlight a serious issue that seems like a scam by Airtel.

I recently recharged with the ₹859 unlimited pack, which includes 2GB data per day and unlimited 5G data. However, I noticed that once I exhaust the 2GB daily quota, my internet stops working—even though I am connected to a 5G network and supposedly entitled to unlimited usage.

When I reached out to Airtel customer care, the executives simply responded that a complaint has been raised and some work is ongoing in the area. But this explanation doesn't make sense—how can there be a network issue in multiple different locations at the same time?

Moreover, when I contacted the 198 helpline, I was told that I had reached my daily data limit, and that’s why the internet was not working. If that’s the case, then what’s the point of calling it unlimited 5G?

This feels like a deliberate misrepresentation and a scam by Airtel. Despite multiple complaints, no resolution has been provided.

I request you to check on your own device as well—see if the same issue happens after using the 2GB daily quota. If this is happening to all of us, then please consider making a video or raising awareness about this. I’m tired of reaching out to Airtel with no results.

They are not taking this issue seriously at all.

comments.text:
@GyanTherapy 
Hospitals often have two price lists: one for insured patients and another for non-insured or cash-paying patients. Insured patients may be charged higher rates, which can be nearly 50% more than the actual cost. If the insurance claim is rejected, the patient may be liable for the additional amount. This practice can lead to unexpected financial burdens on patients.

comments.text: My mother has been doing Insurances from last 15 years and she has had 100 percent claim settlement. First is the company, and according to me Niva Bupa is the worst, whereas HDFC or Star Health have a good claim ratio. However, the main player is the agent, who will empathize with your situation and do everything possible to approve your claim. I saw in this video that you are the one who are talking to Niva Bupa customer care whereas it was you agent's responsibility to talk to them directly in city branch office and he or she provide hard copies to the branch office personally and try to reimburse you claim. And try to never go to the hospitals that do not have cashless because those hospitals are the ones who have done fraud with the patients and companies too.

comments.text:
I have been fortunate to have been reimbursed by Insurance companies three times for my family. One time was for my wife, who went through Laparoscopic surgery for the gall bladder removal. The hospital was a premium one that charged around 1.25 L for the procedure, including a 3-day stay in a Single private room (Usually it costs around 40-50k in a decent hospital). We had to pay just 1500, the rest was paid by "Care Insurance". It was my personal family floater health policy. The second one was for maternity when we were blessed with our daughter. That time it was corporate insurance, and we paid around 7k, and rest of 42k was paid by insurance since it was a co-pay policy. And the recent one was again for my wife for a neuroendocrine tumor procedure. We went for Corporate insurance again, provided by my company. (Though I also had personal health insurance). We paid around 6k and the rest around 1.2 L paid by insurance, including 4-day stay in a single private room. All were cashless claims. 
In my experience, Corporate policy approval was much faster and easier. When I opted for personal insurance claim, they took more time and did in-person verification, and the approval was completed after the procedure was done. In the Corporate policy, we got approval before the procedure was started.

comments.text:
First of all with health insurance what I leared from my experience.

1. Even though if they reject for any reason and you know you are genuine you should go behind them and get that claim settled otherwise in future you won't get any claims.

2. Always read the discharge summary there are chances that a single word could make your claim get rejected for me " with a previous history of so and so disease" was written an my claim got rejected but I took the policy earlier that is before that disease was first identified. If doctor wrote previous history of disease with exact date my claim couldn't have rejected so I went behind it and I requested hospital to give whole treatment report till date and was able to prove that it wasn't a previous existing disease and I got my claim as reimbursement.

3. Chances that your claim is 1 lakh and if doctor can be bribed for 25k and if doctor accepts and writes a discharge summary as if the claim gets rejected then also insurance company is benefitted . It's a chance.

4. For admission and treatment you will get the costliest medicine available their and all available tests done since it's under insurance and that's why the treatment amount varies from hospital to hospital.

5. Even there is a normal waiting period for 2or 3 years within that if any claim raises other than accident such as sugar pressure kidney stone etc you will have to pay no option 

6. Usually we give deceleration as no per existing diseases. Still if you are not sure go for a full body medical checkup and with that report apply for insurance it will make you safer.

7. Get an insurance of no  hidden or copayments or restrictions

8. Get a basic 5lakh insurance + a 45 lakh super topup so that it will be cheaper and you get total 50lakh coverage 

9. No claim settlement will be done on time even if you are discharged by any time it will take again a 3 to 5 hours to get the claim approved since you are not Saif Ali Khan so not safe 

10. If the agent is genuine and he knows every loopholes than collecting commision you are somewhat secure.

11. If opting online insurance agents the. Avoid policy bazar and go with ditto they won't spam you on my personal experience. If it's policy bazar them when they get your phone number you are finished they will keep on calling and you will get frustrated. Id it's ditto they will explain everything and will not force you to take one even if they call you it will be one in a month or two.

12. There is nothing called a best insurance you will know if it was best on not on going for a claim and you will ofcourse learn the hidden criteria for rejection.

13. Maternity isn't an accident or a disease so no claim it's acceptable. 

14. And even if an existing disease wasn't declared at the time of taking insurance and if you complete 5 years with the same then they can't reject on that basis there is government rule.

15. If possible go with 3 year policy together at no cost emi and only in this case you can loot the actual benefit of no cost emi since policy may hike every year and  so the medical expenses.

16. When you get discharged collect the detailed bill usually the hospitals won't give that  they give only summary bill. Thoroughly check the detailed bill and if something you haven't used is mentioned then should clarify with the hospital and leave if they are not cooperating then sent a mail to their official hospital address and ask them to reply it. They won't take risk and you will get your excess amount I got almost 7k like this from hospital

comments.text: A very good informative video! To tackle this, Dr. Devi Shetty, a renowned cardiac surgeon and founder of Narayana Hrudayalaya, initiated the Yeshasvini Health Insurance Scheme to provide affordable healthcare to rural farmers and peasants in Karnataka. This scheme, launched in collaboration with the Karnataka State Government, is managed by the Yeshasvini Cooperative Farmers Health Care Trust.  The scheme is self-funded and NOT linked to any insurance company. The Yeshasvini Health Insurance Scheme operates on a cooperative model, where members pay a minimal annual premium, giving them access to cashless surgeries at empaneled hospitals. Since Narayana Hrudayalaya directly manages approvals without third-party insurers, it reduces fraudulent claims and unnecessary costs, ensuring funds are used efficiently for genuine medical needs.

comments.text: IRDAI is just a puppet. They just forward your complaint to the GRO of insurance company, and then even if GRO and insurance company keeps sleeping on the complaint, IRDAI is not bothered. You send the mail multiple times to IRDAI, they will just keep on forwarding the mail to GRO of insurance company. More than this there is no authority to IRDAI for complaint resolution, or maybe they don't want to make any efforts for that. Added to this IRDAI does not have an escalation/grievance matrix on their website to reach higher levels of IRDAI, which means you just get stuck with IRDAI lower level. They don;t even have any defined timelines for complaint resolution.

HDFC ERGO outrightly rejected my mother's genuine medical reimbursement claim amounting to almost Rs4,50,000/ for her varicose vein treatment citing a vague reason that her condition was a preexisting one although she had coverage of 5 lakhs.

The IRDAI should implement strict regulatory protocols and  tighten their grip on these shameless,heartless insurance companies and impose heavy penalties with interest for defrauding their clients and causing immense mental and psychological harassment.Its a huge scam in this country.

comments.text:
I am a student, and my father bought me this Realme Narzo 70 Turbo after I requested it Him many times. Since I had used Realme before, I trusted the brand and bought the phone without much research, only to realize later that they are dumping phones with minor changes.


Now, I can’t even resell it because if I ask my father for another phone, he will say, "You insisted for five years that you wanted a new phone, and now that you have it, you want a different one?"


This situation is frustrating, and many users are facing the same issue.


So I bought the Realme Narzo 70 Turbo in November. Before this, I used the Realme 3 for almost five years. Previously, Realme was very good, providing fast updates, but now I have noticed that they are dumping phones.


For example, after launching the Realme Narzo 70 Turbo, they released the same phone with a different design and named it the Realme P1 Speed, which confuses many users and makes them feel guilty, thinking the new phone is better. Similarly, they launched the Realme P3 Pro and P3 Ultra.


In Realme UI 5.0, they advertised the Trinity Engine, but in Realme UI 6.0, they removed it. The default touch sampling rate is limited to 180Hz, and even in GT mode, it only goes up to 240Hz, putting restrictions on the phone so that users feel the need to buy a new one.


Now, updates are also delayed. In November, I received the October update, in December, I got the November update, and then in March, I received the January update.


The latest update has ruined battery life—previously, I got 7-8 hours of backup on normal usage, but now it has dropped to 4-5 hours. The battery percentage from 20-100% has become unpredictable, and charging speed has also been reduced. The speaker sound has also become lower.


Additionally, the issue of mistouch is still not fixed. There are bloatware apps in system applications that cannot be removed, like Chinese ID—why do Indians even need a Chinese ID? There are even more Chinese apps pre-installed that cannot be removed, which are also used in China.


Another major issue is "oms-core," which cannot be disabled, removed, or uninstalled. Even if you turn off notifications, they automatically turn back on. It also has installation permissions, and if you turn them off, they automatically re-enable themselves.


Many users feel guilty after buying a phone, seeing a slightly better Realme phone launching soon after, so they resell their phone and buy the new one, only to realize later that another new phone has been released, repeating the cycle.


No YouTuber is talking about this issue. They just unbox phones for views, even though the new phones are almost the same, just with a design change and similar or lower prices.


Even in updates, there are issues. For example, the Realme 14X got fast updates Before Launching of Realme P3X After That Realme 14x started facing update delays.


Please make a video on this, sir!
comments.author: @TYETENTRON

comments.text:
I purchased health insurance from Star Health Insurance for my entire family—my parents, sister, and myself. Two months later, they emailed me claiming my father had diabetes mellitus and its complications, which were not declared at the time of the proposal. But I had already declared it, and it's clearly mentioned in the policy document.  

What’s shocking is that I never even applied for a claim—so how did they suddenly ‘discover’ his condition? Because I had declared it myself. Yet, they accused me of non-disclosure and canceled his coverage, removing him from the policy just 4-5 days after sending the mail. I reached out for clarification, but they haven’t even responded.  

This is how health insurance companies operate.
comments.author: @adityakushwaha6385

comments.text:
This is extremely scary that an influencer has to go through so much trouble to get his claim passed. A normal person without any social media reach will have no chance to get his claim passed unless he has contacts higher up. This is reality of India. Nothing gets done here without having contacts or influence. Both public and private enterprises are equally corrupt.

One thing I should mention, don't just look at the Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR) as it includes cases where partial amounts were paid, thus distorting the figure. CSR should be used in conjunction with Incurred Claim Ratio (ICR) which shows total amount of claims paid by an insurer against the total premium collected. If both metrics are healthy, then only go for that Insurance company.

I always stick to govt health insurers because CSR and ICR are good for them. Govt health insurers will delay your claims a lot and frustrate you but in the end, you will get it. For cashless, it's less of a hassle but again approvals may get delayed. Main thing is you will get your claim, even if it takes 6-12 months. I only claimed once and it took 4 months to process but I got the money in the end. They were even forgiving of some missing documents which no private insurer will allow.

comments.text:
There is no remedy for this, If you have Cashless, Hospitals will deliberately try to increase the bill and then insurance companies blames the customer for creating fake bills. 

In case of reimbursement, they will never allow any claims, whatever be the reason, in my father's case, even though it was a port policy so thers was no waiting period but they rejected the first operation's claim saying that the wait period is not over. In case of the second operation when the official wait period was over, they rejected saying that my father used to drink, but that was not true. We gave them the proof, then they said he has diabetes which was not true, we gave the proof, theu said you had leg pain 5 years back but that was a minor pain and wasn't a cause for the issue, we had the proof for that as well. They harassed us for 4 months and then rejected. The agent blocked our numbers, and we became helpless, it has been a year and my father is in so much depression as he had to spend 10L and the hospital anyways imcreased the bill deliberately charging us for a single room even when my father was in a 6 bed dorm.

TATA AIG has ruined our lives, we have already tried everything since last 1.5 years but nothing happened. We can't keep on fighting as that is another money drain...


Everyone is the same

comments.text:
In India, Insurance is a BIG Scam for the Middle Class. Insurance Companies are FOR-PROFIT Businesses. The ONLY Two Ways to Make Profits are Selling More Policies and Denying Claims. 
While Selling Insurance these Businesses are VEY Polite and Courteous. But these SAME Businesses become very cold and distant when you file a claim. 
Do note: Insurance is a subject matter of "Solicitation", which essentially means you can be legally fooled, deceived, or scammed by Sellers/Agents. 
Finally, Rakesh HAD to rely on or flex his "Celebrity Status" to get the claim of a mere Rs. 75K passed. Just imagine the scenario of a Middle-Class, Salaried person who has filed a claim of a few lakh Rupees.

comments.text:
Maine 2 mahina research kiya tha insurance lene se pehle, mera experience batata hu

Maine policy bazaar se insurance liya hai care health insurance
Mujhe dengue hua tha to maine policy bazaar me baat kiya to vo bole hi "haa aap admit ho" sakte ho claim mil jaayega

To mai directly kahin bhi admit nahi hua
Sabse pehle apne usual doctor ke paas gaya jinke clinic me normally jaate rehta hu, 
To unko bola ki admit hona hai, to aise normally doctor ke contacts hote hai sab jagah to unhone 4-5 jagah baat kiya, prices nikale sab jagah ke aur care health insurance ka cashless bhi pucha, kyuki kabhi kabhi cashless hospital list me dikhata hai lekin actually me hota nahi hai, 
Fir unhone proper deal kiya hospital se ki mera patient aa rha hai, jitna bhi insurance se reimbursement milega utna hi aapko denge usse ₹1 extra nahi,
To admit kiye doctor ke trust or reference se, 1 week ke baad discharge hua, koi upfront payment nahi karna pada, aur hospital ne intimation kiya health insurance se, unhone ne hi mail kiya saare documents ke saath, sablog mail me the cc me the saare follow ups transparency ke saath tha,

Mera bill around 49k bana tha, lekin claim sirf 39k hi mila, lekin yahi deal hua tha to mujhe kuch bhi extra pay nahi karna pada
Abhi pura claim nahi milta hai, vo kuch kuch cheeje vo cover nahi karte.

Mere bhai ka bhu leg fracture hua tha to surgery karna tha, aur surgery matlab 1 lakh kahin nhi gaya, to humne wahi kiya, sidha doctor ke paas gaye vo apne reference me har jagah ka price pata kiye aur 60k me sab ho gaya.

Mere doctor ka aisa rehta hai ki mera patient itne price me hospital se bahar ho jaana chahiye ₹1 bhi extra nahi

To ye pro tip hai ki apne family doctor se jaan pehchaan rakho aur unke references ka use karo. Fixed price decide kar ke hospital me admit karo aur policy bazaar se hi insurance lo, best hai
comments.author: @surajcreative7009

comments.text:
I am a Law Officer at The New India Assurance, the largest government-owned non-life insurance company in India and Southeast Asia. We lead the health insurance segment, among others. Being a government-owned company, we do not reject claims arbitrarily.

My advice: never opt for standalone health insurance companies. These companies are notorious for rejecting claims. Just check their monthly claim settlement ratios on Google.

Once, I attended a hearing at the Insurance Ombudsman’s office. Out of 10 cases scheduled during that time slot, 9 were against Niva Bupa. A senior officer at the Ombudsman’s office (who was from our company, serving deputation at Ombudsman office) graciously allowed me to observe other proceedings, and I witnessed this company rejecting claims on highly technical and flimsy grounds.

Avoid these insurers: Star Health Insurance (infamously notorious), Niva Bupa, and Care Health.

comments.text: As an insurance agent myself i always say to my customer to always opt for cashless treatment no matter what in health and cashless repairs for cars. Hospitals are mostly corrupt as they overcharge from customers in case of non cashless treatment. In cashless cases there is an agreement between hospital and insurance company that they will not charge patients more than the agreed amount for the surgery or any kind of treatment. But in many hospitals they do send bills more than the agreed amount to insurance companies. By then the insurance companies do the deduction in the bill and the rest of the amount has to be paid by the patient. Reimbursement claims are the worst as u will never get the same amount submitted to the insurance companies because of the overcharging in the bills by the hospitals. Be aware. Always opt for cashless and always read terms and conditions before purchasing a health or life insurance policy. HEALTH INSURANCE IS NOT A SCAM!!!!
comments.author: @sumit6600

comments.text: If "HEALTH INSURANCE IS NOT A SCAM", then why is the patient asked to pay the amount that is overcharged by the hospital, why shouldn't insurance companies pay the amount ?
comments.author: @UvUtkarsh

comments.text: @ bcoz there is a tie up between hospital and insurance companies that they will not charge over the agreed amount. But in non cashless hospitals add many items for instance they will add registration charges and they will hike surgeons fees and much more. They know that insurance companies will only reimburse tie up amount and rest will be paid by the customer. This is the win win situation for hospitals as they will not give discharge summary to customers until every penny is paid.
comments.author: @sumit6600

comments.text: Suppose a Genuine patient who has a health ins.  needs a surgery   in the Hospital where Insurance company don't Provide Cash less  service  but Doctors and surgeons are good there. In that case what would the Customer do.
comments.author: @localstories3404

comments.text: @localstories3404  In that case insurance company reimburse the amount after all necessary documents are submitted to the insurance company. But the insurance company will only reimburse the amount fixed for the specific disease. There is an agreement to curb hospitals from overcharging. For example a simple cataract surgery goes for around 30k to 40k(depending on region) which include lens, surgery, surgeon visit charges, consumables may or may not be included etc etc but the hospital charges way more than the fixed amount. Like more than 50-60k which a patient has to pay before being discharged. The hospital will hike the surgeon's fees in doubles. Not all hospitals do this but many do. At the end the patient has to suffer and he/ she will blame insurance companies for not reimburse the full amount. I have many cases in which the patient didn't get the full amount. Hospitals are very clever and they are always searching for the patients like these. This is the reason I always ask to go for cashless no matter what. Hope u got my point.
comments.author: @sumit6600

comments.text: Another case At the time of Buying policy  too many hospitals are tied up but after sometime Compny slowly delists some good Hospitals.
comments.author: @localstories3404

comments.text: @localstories3404  They delist them for a reason. A broken agreement, complaints from patients, renaming of the hospital, bank change and many other reasons could be there. It could be vice versa like the insurance company denied the claim, payment issue to hospitals.

comments.text:
My father does health check up every year, this year, doc noticed something wrong and suggested Angiogram, in that found a couple of blocks in the heart..

Now I initiated the claim.. now insurance rejected claim, the reason is, they want the doctor letter stating when the heart issue started? I was like what?? Who would know when the issue started, if my father hasn't gone for the regular check up, we would have never know there is an issue with his heart.. I went through mental turmoil for a few days.. 
Doc said, how would I know when the problem started, I only noticed it when your father came to be for general check up.. how can I give a letter for something I don't know..

God the lengths these insurance companies going to deny the claims..
Finally after many days of to and fro, got the claim approved

comments.text:
As an Advocate I would say that it is common for insurance companies to reduce the amount of your claim. As you always have the option to move to a court for a better claim. But in court it would take almost 6-10 years for claim but you will get true value of your claim in the court. The best way I would recommend is 
1)Ask for claim from the company itself and if you are satisfied with the claim(not want to fight 10 years in court) than keep it.
2) If not satisfied with the claim than move to court and try yo compromise with the insurance company for a better claim. If you are satisfied with the claim than file a compromise petition and settle the matter in lok adalat.
3) If you are still not satisfied with the claim than fight in court it might take3-10 years though.
Hope this helps😊

I work as a Business Development Manager in a well known INSURANCE COMPANY, and i totally agree with you sir.. There is a thing called outmost good faith.. where they gonna believe everything you are saying while making a contract so always keep that in mind to be 100% crystal clear while signing for an Insurance and most importantly if you every want to buy any kind of insurance policy and you know someone who is a agent, no matter how good or bad he/she is just ask them to meet there D/O or MANAGER and then discuss with them regarding what you want. Agents get standard commission of 15% on health insurance and 35% on life insurance and these companies also launch contest so add 20% more reward opportunities for them.

In the fiscal year 2023-2024, several Indian health insurance companies reported notable profits:

Star Health & Allied Insurance: Achieved a net profit of ₹845 crore, marking a 37% increase from the previous year's ₹619 crore. 

Care Health Insurance: Reported a net profit of ₹305 crore. 

Niva Bupa Health Insurance: Registered a net profit of ₹85 crore. 

ICICI Lombard General Insurance: For the quarter ending December 31, 2024, the company reported a profit after tax of ₹724 crore, a 68% increase from the previous year. 

Reliance General Insurance: In the quarter ending March 2024, the company reported a net profit of ₹58.31 crore. 


Overall, the net profits of health insurers grew to ₹917 crore in FY 2024 from ₹457 crore in the previous year, reflecting a 100% growth.

comments.text: Thank you soooo much bhaiya for making this video. It's going to help millions. One more tip - if you fall ill and doctor detects some disease which is not so serious right now, and gets cured also in a few weeks/months like TB, Uveitis, skin infection, etc. then please send that info to the insurance company as your health update. So that in future, when you claim for something else, and doctor mentions the old disease in your patient history, the insurance company cannot reject the claim saying you didn't inform us about this disease. Send the insurance company all the prescriptions and lab reports as soon as you recover from that disease to avoid future claim rejections! The best way to get insurance claims is by being aware like you said bhaiya!!

comments.text:
Being an insurance agent I'll share my opinion. The person who is buying the insurance should learn more before buying the insurance or atleast learn just after buying so as to know what claims a person can get. Ask more and more questions to your agent. Don't pay the premium just because the agent is your uncle.
In the case where there is 100% genuine claim, there is no reason for the company to hold it. But when companies find a non declared disease or they get a reimbursement claim from hospitals that are already in cashless tie-up.  They see the chances of fraud either by the customer or the hospital.
There is a mentality in customers that once they have paid the premium, now everything is upon the company.  But very few times they realise that it a situation or requirement base contract. Hence, the company will fulfil the paperwork and then allow the calim to be passed. 

Lastly, there are solutions for the claims that are not passed. There exists a Beema Lokpal, which is an Ombudsman office for insurance. But you need complete clarification from the company on what grounds the claim is rejected even after multiple emails and explanations. Then you can proceed towards the Lokpal office.

comments.text:
A Big Thanks to you for making video on this sensitive topic. Most social influencers would've probably avoided this thinking it may affect their future claim settlement.

You have covered all the points pretty well but I would add one important point which was missed. Have you noticed that almost all Health Insurance has Pre-existing disease related restrictions and waiting periods but never ask us to go for a medical examination before purchase? Why do they rely on us to specify these data truthfully? Even Auto insurance companies do physical verification of vehicle if previous policy has lapsed by even a day and you try to buy a new one.
I guess they do it for 2 reasons. Both go in their favour. 1st - Insurance selling is faster due to not having this step. 2nd - Future Claim rejection is easier if customers forget something to declare during purchase. I honestly missed declaring one such condition for one member while buying Niva Bupa policy for my family (Me, spouse, Two kids). When trying to avail cashless for a totally unrelated disease/treatment this condition was mentioned in one of the submitted documents and Niva Bupa not only denied the claim but also cancelled the policy citing suppression of facts.  Mine was also a 3 years advance premium paid policy. Thankfully I had corporate mediclaim else all 4 members would've been without Insurance until we buy a new one.
They rely on us making mistake on answering all those medical questions and specifying each and every medical details.
If I being an educated guy can make such mistake how would all those less educated or aged folks wouldn't make some mistake filling out so many Medical questions and details?

comments.text:
Great video!

I want to share my experience what I faced during cataract surgery of my mother. 

As I had corporate health insurance from MEDI ASSIST for myself and parents, I tried to go for cashless facility. But before going for the claim I took my mother for a check up in the same hospital (ILS Hospital, Agartala). The senior eye specialist checked her and assured it's matter of a oneday that the whole operation procedure will be completed. When I asked about the cost for the operation,he said within 21,22k everything will be completed. Then I went for the cashless claim section for enrollment and filled all details of the insurance. Next day I received mail from Medi Assist that the claim has been submitted and after oneday I received another mail that my claim is approved. Here comes the most shocking thing!

I was surprised to see the hospital submitted bill of 40k for the same cataract surgery. Out of the whole bill only 22k has been approved and remaining 18k I need to pay for which they mentioned extra-medicinal expences and copay. After that as a Aam Aadmi I felt better to do the surgery in a near govt hospital where the operation was free and few medicines around 2k I had to purchase. With God's grace the operation went well and she recovered within few weeks.

I don't know who's was the culprit behind this the insurance provider or the hospital...but in between these, genuine Aam Aadmi with genuine claim gets deprived... 😢

Cashless is hassle-free but there is a cons in this system if the insurance company or hospital is too much towards corruption. 
As far we have experienced, the same hospital billed less when we went for reimbursement but charged way more when billed for cashless.

For example, if my sum insured is exhausted in 5-6 times of hospital checkups/treatment under reimbursement case
The same treatment under cashless transaction will wipe out the sum insured in just around 2 times

The treatment was chemotherapy for brain tumour stage 3/4

comments.text:
Recently I took Health insurance for my parents from Aditya Birla Health insurance company, and within 6 months of policy purchase my dad was admitted to hospital due to random cough and lung infection, I went to a tie-up hospital and admitted my dad for 1 week and proceeded with cashless process. Total bill for 7 days was 50k which was almost double if I had paid money myself and then done reimbursement, but my claim was fully processed and didn't have to pay any thing, even though consumables were not covered in the policy as we have to take a rider for it which I didn't opted while purchasing policy but then too the hospital didn't ask for the money as they were already charged double from the insurance company. 

There was another experience were I have applied for claim. Once I was admitted in the hospital for a surgery that time I was not having my personal health insurance policy but I had my office health insurance policy of future genrali company. But the problem was that it had 20% co-pay. This co-pay is a biggest scam in health insurance.I opted for cashless facility in the hospital, my bill was of 70K but due to co-pay scam I got only 20k as cashless amount and hospital asked me to pay remaining 50k but I said I don't have money, they said will discuss with their sir what we can do, then they asked ro pay 30k, I told I have only 15k, they took some time but agreed to take 15k. Means see howmuch they are over charging. Now I jave purchased my personal health insurance policy without any co-pay scam

These were my two health insurance claim experience.

comments.text:
Nice informative video Rakesh, i work as a health insurance agent. Whatever you see saying is 100% correct. Common ppl aren't as lucky as you & also there is an issue with agents who seldom have limited knowledge. I generally advise my customers that they have to be patient in reimbursement & if by chance any money is deducted they need not worry because there is a process it gets every single penny back without getting so much in to these things.

Anyone who had to be admitted in hospital & had to undergo reimbursement than submit all the papers, keep all photocopied. If your money is deducted put it in grievance department. Wait for 30 days of you don't get positive response from the company, make a complaint in OMBUDSMAN.

If your complaint is admitted in OMBUDSMAN you will receive every single penny back from company & of its delayed you can also ask for interest.

comments.text:
My experience with Tata AIG health insurance was good, which I think so that was just because of it being cashless. As far as I have experienced, cashless is the best way to get insurance claim and be tension-free. Most of the claims which are held for so long, rejected, or are partially reimbursed are those who were not eligible for cashless claim. These companies deduct huge amounts on the basis of nothing.
On the other hand, Tata AIG in car insurance is rejecting my claim on the basis of some ownership change, which is totally stupid. The insurance is for car and not the person whose name is in the insurance policy of that car. Will definitely reach out to higher authorities regarding this. But have very little hope that anyone would hear it. We spend literally lakhs in these types of insurance policies, but when it comes to claim, these companies refuse to reimburse even thousands. Sad reality.
comments.author: @hardiksethi20

comments.text:
Insurance profits from fear, creating dependence on potential misfortunes.

It benefits companies more than those it protects. At its core, insurance is economic rent—you pay not for innovation or productivity but to avoid a major expense. Yet, in aggregate, it's a losing deal, as insurers collect more than they pay out.

Ideally, the government should manage it. But since the risk of a large expense is too high for individuals, people are forced to participate—even in an exploitative system.

Insurance is economic rent.


I'll buy insurance too.

I would, but only because I have to. It's highly exploitative. It's a service that should be run by the government. While there’s a risk of facing a large expense, overall, you’re likely to pay more in premiums than what you receive from that risk. For an individual, that risk can feel overwhelming, but if everyone contributes, then collectively the risk is lower.

comments.text:
Health insurance is a scam in every country. I've been living in California my whole life, and since childhood, I've been under health insurance as mandated by the government. After turning 18, I started paying for it myself, whereas earlier, my parents used to cover the costs.  

Two years ago, I had an accident—not critical—and the medical bill was approximately $4,000. When I filed a claim with my insurance company, they only covered 20% of the bill, despite the fact that I pay $250 monthly for coverage. Health insurance is just a scam run by big companies.

comments.text:
The “private” word you wrote itself means what you say. 
This is a capitalist world my friend. Money is the biggest motivator. 
Good things cost money. Out of all doctors, you would want to get your family treated in hands of best doctor. Now that comes at a cost. No one works, or should be expected to work for less than what he deserves. 
If govt says that all doctors should be paid equal salary to cap the cost of treatment, there’ll be no motivation for doctors to work hard and be the best in the town. The health care quality will degrade. 

And to be honest, talking for majority, neither doctors of private hospital, neither professors of private colleges nor teachers of private schools are becoming millionaires. They are salaried employees. May be a bit higher than what they may be paid else where, but they are still mostly salaried. 
It’s the business owners who make fortunes out of them. 

Corporate hospitals when bills someone, includes just a small fraction of doctors charge, major portion includes your room charges, consumables (sold at inflated prices), and other charges which aren’t always purely medical. 
I don’t know where you are working, but if you’re working as a salaried employee, your salary comes from the money end consumer pays your company. If you expect your salary to rise, in whatever sector, so will rise the cost born by the end consumer. 
If at all govt wants to regulate price, they should control the highly inflated price of consumables and pharmaceuticals. Just find the buying price of a iv cannula per piece by the hospital vs the mrp vs the price they are selling at. This is just an example.

comments.text:
**"Samsung’s after-sales service is a scam. I faced the infamous green line issue, which is a known manufacturing defect, yet Samsung initially refused to repair it under warranty. When I repeatedly asked for the exact policy under which they denied my claim, they dodged the question, only saying ‘T&C apply.’ Even the Customer Experience Manager, Anchal Negi, refused to provide a valid explanation and just tried to negotiate without transparency.

At first, they completely denied any repair. But after escalating the issue to Samsung India HQ via email, they finally agreed to reduce the repair cost, and I had to pay ₹2088. However, this amount shouldn’t have been charged at all. Many others are forced to pay the full repair cost simply because they don’t know how to escalate or fight back.

Samsung is scamming customers by rejecting warranty claims without clear reasons, especially for widespread issues like the green line defect. More people need to be aware of their rights and not fall for their tactics!
comments.author: @AryanKumarSingh-y3w

comments.text: Okay, so I just have to say this Niva Bupa actually did an amazing job with my claim. Like, I got LASIK done at Shroff Eye Centre in Delhi, and the total cost was ₹95,000. And you know how insurance companies usually make you run around, ask for a million documents, and still find a way to delay or reject your claim? Yeah, none of that happened. They approved my cashless claim in one go. Just like that. No drama, no unnecessary back-and-forth. But here’s the part that really surprised me I had OPD visits and pre-surgery checkups before the LASIK, and they reimbursed those too. And get this I actually went to another hospital first to get my LASIK tests done before finalizing Shroff, and even that claim got approved. I was fully expecting them to reject it or at least create some issue, but nope. Smooth process, no headaches. I know a lot of people have bad experiences with insurance claims, so maybe I just got lucky. But in my case, Niva Bupa handled everything better than I expected. No complaints at all.

comments.text:
Sir, my father had an accident on May 15, 2024. Some people admitted him to the hospital, and when I found out about it, we immediately rushed to the hospital. The hospital staff demanded ₹10,000 from us, saying it was the emergency admission charge. They later generated a total bill of ₹45,000.

I come from a lower-middle-class family, and I had to arrange the money to pay the bill. To repay that amount, I had to leave my studies and start working. My father didn’t even suffer major injuries.

I had filed a reimbursement claim with ESIC, but I haven't received any money yet. The employees there are not giving any proper response. Once, they told me that ₹6,000 would be reimbursed, but it has been almost a year, and I haven't received anything.

ESIC collects money from us, but it’s of no use. If you visit any hospital, you won’t find a single patient saying they are receiving proper treatment through ESIC.
comments.author: @PriyanshuKumar-m3r2j

comments.text: भारत में आजकल स्वास्थ्य बीमा की वास्तविकता कई मायनों में जटिल और चुनौतीपूर्ण बन गई है। यहाँ इसकी स्थिति को कुछ प्रमुख बिंदुओं के माध्यम से समझा जा सकता है:कम पहुंच और जागरूकता: भारत में अभी भी बड़ी आबादी, खासकर ग्रामीण इलाकों में, स्वास्थ्य बीमा से वंचित है। केवल 30-40% लोग ही किसी न किसी रूप में बीमा कवरेज के दायरे में आते हैं। जागरूकता की कमी और जटिल पॉलिसी शर्तों के कारण लोग इसे अपनाने में हिचकते हैं।प्रीमियम में बढ़ोतरी: हाल के वर्षों में स्वास्थ्य बीमा के प्रीमियम में तेजी से वृद्धि हुई है। चिकित्सा मुद्रास्फीति (मेडिकल इन्फ्लेशन) और बढ़ती स्वास्थ्य सेवाओं की लागत के कारण प्रीमियम 10-25% तक सालाना बढ़ रहा है। कई लोगों के लिए यह बोझ बनता जा रहा है, खासकर मध्यम वर्ग के लिए।18% जीएसटी का बोझ: स्वास्थ्य बीमा प्रीमियम पर 18% जीएसटी लगाया जाता है, जो इसे और महंगा बनाता है। यह मध्यम वर्ग और निम्न आय वर्ग के लिए एक बड़ी बाधा है, क्योंकि वे पहले से ही बढ़ते खर्चों से जूझ रहे हैं। पिछले कुछ वर्षों में सरकार ने इससे 21,000 करोड़ रुपये से अधिक की कमाई की है, लेकिन आम लोगों को राहत नहीं मिली।क्लेम सेटलमेंट की समस्या: निजी स्वास्थ्य बीमा में क्लेम सेटलमेंट एक बड़ी चुनौती है। कई बार बीमा कंपनियां तकनीकी आधार पर क्लेम खारिज कर देती हैं, जिससे लोगों का भरोसा टूटता है। कॉर्पोरेट या सरकारी योजनाओं को छोड़कर, व्यक्तिगत पॉलिसीधारकों को अक्सर परेशानी का सामना करना पड़ता है।चिकित्सा खर्चों का दबाव: भारत में स्वास्थ्य सेवाओं पर व्यक्तिगत खर्च (आउट-ऑफ-पॉकेट एक्सपेंस) बहुत अधिक है, जो कुल चिकित्सा व्यय का लगभग 60% है। एक बड़ी बीमारी या अस्पताल में भर्ती होने की स्थिति में मध्यम वर्ग के परिवारों की बचत खत्म हो सकती है, और कई बार कर्ज लेना पड़ता है। स्वास्थ्य बीमा होने के बावजूद, पूर्ण कवरेज न मिलने से यह समस्या बनी रहती है।
